headings.text: Need CNC Machined Parts in PEEK?

meta_description: PEEK is an advanced engineering plastic with good temperature resistance, biocompatibility, and chemical resistance. Learn about manufacturing custom PEEK parts.

sections.heading: Generic PEEK
sections.text: Generic PEEK Machining PEEK allows for a wide array of applications that take advantage of this materialâs exceptional properties. PEEK has very few downsides when compared to other engineering plastics. One advantage that sets it apart is its heat resistance. It can operate at high temperatures for longer than most other plastics. Additionally, PEEK is hydrophobic, so it does not readily absorb water, and it also outgasses very little under vacuum conditions. This stability is a great asset. PEEKâs uses include medical applications for temporary implants all the way to aerospace components exposed to extreme vacuum.

sections.heading: USP Class VI PEEK (TECAPEEK)
sections.text: USP Class VI PEEK (TECAPEEK) PEEK rated for USP Class VI, such as TECAPEEK MT, are biocompatible variants specifically designed for use with medical technology. It is characterized by very high chemical resistance, resistance to various sterilization methods, and ability to retain its mechanical properties in high temperatures. Medical grade peek is often used for medical applications and devices where limited contact with skin and tissue will be needed. USP Class VI PEEK is also desirable for medical use due to its ability to sustain many sterilization cycles without degrading.

sections.heading: Peek GF30
sections.text: Peek GF30 PEEK GF30 is a 30% glass filled variant of PEEK. Compared to generic unfilled PEEK, GF30 has superior rigidity and increased mechanical strength. Glass reinforced PEEK also has increased dimensional stability and creep strength. This material is well suited for parts that experience high static loads in higher temperatures. PEEK GF30 is less suitable for bearing or wear applications due to the abrasive effect the glass fibers have on mating surfaces.

sections.heading: Finishes
sections.text: Finishes PEEK parts are naturally an opaque grey or beige. Given PEEKâs polymeric structure, paints and coatings are difficult to bond to its surface. However, thanks to its inherent chemical resistance, it is often acceptable to leave parts as machined. As Machined : PEEK is easily machined and can achieve smooth surface finishes. Xometry offers auto-quoting of surface roughnesses as small as 32uin Ra for PEEK but can quote even lower values with a manual review.Â Bead Blast : Any undesirable tool marks may be removed by mechanically abrading the surface with glass beads.

sections.heading: Cost-saving Design Tips
sections.text: Cost-saving Design Tips Due to the materialâs high cost, manufacturing overhead costs must be minimized. One method to help manage the cost of CNC machining in PEEK is by requesting annealing during fabrication. This may reduce surface and internal cracking, which will reduce scrap rate and reduce overall costs for the manufacturer. Other design strategies can be found in our CNC Machining Design Guide .
